reason: The score and justifications are highly accurate. The HP Wiki's 'Behind the scenes' section confirms: the forest scene (Crouch staggering in delusionally, mistaking a tree for Percy/Weatherby, begging to see Dumbledore) is cut - instead Harry and co. simply find his dead body. The entire mystery subplot around his disappearance is eliminated. Winky is cut entirely, removing the Quidditch World Cup confrontation and his abusive dismissal of her. The film makes him 'somewhat more cheerful and less ruthless' and shows Dumbledore saying it 'destroyed Crouch to send his son to Azkaban' - the opposite of the book where he showed no remorse. Percy's role is also cut, removing the 'Weatherby' dynamic. The one minor quibble: the scorer says his 'midnight break-in to Snape's office' is cut - this is actually Crouch Jr. disguised as Moody, not Crouch Sr., so that specific claim is slightly confused. But it doesn't materially affect the score. The 62/100 total and the dimension breakdown (personality and narrative role hit hardest, arc nearly gutted) are well-supported by what the film actually does to the character.
